meaning: n.阵风,小雪,小雨,飓风,慌张vt.使恐慌,使狼狈vi.激动,慌张
examples: Her arrival caused a flurry of excitement.
These young fellows are all in a flurry.
The recent flurry of M&A deals continued, with several new tie-ups surfacing.
Or perhaps, they chose to stay hidden from the daily flurry of human activity.
The Flynns broke ground on the casino in October 1999, touching off a flurry of litigation and inquiry.
